sections.paragraph:
πικρῶς adv. of πικρός (Aeschyl. et al.; pap e.g. PPetr III p. 115, 42 H (8)f, 8 [III b.c.]; LXX, TestSol, TestAbr A; JosAs, ApcEsdr; Ar. 12, 2; Mel., P. 26, 181) bitterly, fig. (Diod S 3, 71, 3 of the painful oppression of Cronus’ rule; Appian, Liby. 100 §472 π. κολάζειν=punish severely; Jos., Ant. 9, 118 βλασφημεῖν) κλαίειν (Is 22:4; 33:7; TestAbr A 11 p. 90, 6 [Stone p. 28]; JosAs 10:17; ApcrEsdr 5:8 p. 30, 1 Tdf.) weep bitterly Mt 26:75; Lk 22:62; GJs 13:1, 3; 15:3. ὁ λέων … ὠρύετο πικρῶς the lion roared ferociously AcPl 2, 7 Ha.—M-M. TW.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 813
